changed.from: Handle: cross-fit estimators \widehat b^{(-f)} and \widehat p^{(-f)}; derive a primitive margin that stabilizes the cutpoint map p_{m\ell}\mapsto A_m^P; solve the primal-dual linear programs defining \overline S_-(P) and \epsilon^\dagger(P); and compose their dual multipliers with held-out residuals for b_{d\ell im} and p_{m\ell}.
changed.to: Handle: on each training fold, round \widehat p^{(-f)}_{m\ell} to its nearest grid point q_s, aggregate or select one arm for each represented cutpoint, and estimate \alpha_m and the arm-specific factual outcome cells. Calibrate these cells to the fixed-threshold Roy factual-law polytope (equivalently, recover the cellwise transport margins by adjacent differences) and express that polytope with a fixed full-row-rank equality system obtained by deleting redundant marginal and duplicate-arm equations. Solve the reduced primal-dual LP for \overline S_-(P), form its cross-fitted one-step estimator by composing the unique reduced dual/envelope gradient with held-out finite-cell residuals, and set \widehat\epsilon^\dagger=(\kappa_0-\widehat{\overline S}_-)_+. The rounded threshold has no first-order score on the correct-classification event. At primal or dual ties, estimate the active-face directional derivative of the reduced LP and apply a directional numerical-delta/bootstrap procedure.
changed.reason: Cutpoint saturation places every true p_{m\ell} exactly on a q_s, so the raw indicator 1\{q_k\le\widehat p_{m\ell}\} is not stabilized by any positive distance-to-cutpoint margin. For example, at an interior p=q_s a regular sample proportion falls on either side with asymptotic probability near one half, causing an entire resistance cell to enter or leave A_m^P. Nearest-grid rounding is consistent because the finite grid has positive separation. In addition, raw empirical b generally violates the exact Roy/mass identities, and the displayed equality system is redundant, so an uncalibrated plug-in LP may be infeasible and cannot generically have a unique dual. Calibration and an irredundant reduced LP are therefore formula-level corrections required before the inference OEQ can be proved.

changed.to: W_1,\ldots,W_n are i.i.d. with law P, and the F cross-fitting folds form a partition with n_f/n\to\gamma_f\in(0,1) for every f.
changed.reason: A sample law, fold-size stability, and held-out independence are required to derive the finite-cell central limit theorem and the one-step expansion.
Together with the strictly separated finite q-grid, this makes nearest-grid rounding recover every true cutpoint index with probability tending to one.

changed.to: In the representative-cutpoint reduced factual parameterization, every nonstructurally-zero recovered row and column margin is strictly positive and every rank-shape inequality holds strictly.
changed.reason: This makes the calibration map locally regular and keeps small empirical perturbations inside the same feasible factual-law face for the Gaussian branch.

changed.to: The full-row-rank reduced LP defining \overline S_-(P) has a unique primal optimizer, a unique reduced dual optimizer, a nonsingular active-constraint basis, and strict complementarity.
changed.reason: These conditions make the LP value locally affine and its envelope gradient single-valued, which is the regular Gaussian branch requested by the OEQ.

The strict inequality keeps the positive-part map in \epsilon^\dagger=(\kappa_0-\overline S_-)_+ locally linear; at equality its limit is generally one-sided rather than Gaussian even when the LP optimizer is unique.

changed.to: The influence function obtained by composing the reduced-LP envelope gradient with the finite-cell observed-data scores has variance \sigma^2 satisfying 0<\sigma^2<\infty.
changed.reason: A positive finite variance is needed for a nondegenerate Gaussian normalization and Wald coverage.
